title.en: 为什么人们通常放弃学习高级公式集和高级CFOP

content.en:
为什么人们通常放弃学习高级公式集和高级CFOP

Jayden McNeill

无论是VLS, ZBLL, 伪入槽, 甚至是F<RU>F'这样的东西,绝大多数人开始尝试学习这几种还原方法, 但最终都出于各种原因放弃了.

有时候理由是合理的, 比如如果他们知道一个能够让他们很轻松地计划十字或提高他们的指法, 比掌握小鱼ZBLL有着更高很多的投资回报率 (ROI) 的这个事实.

不过, 有一个我在拍摄第一批 ''少即是多'' (''Less is more'') 系列视频并且想着用ZBLL做2GLL H和仅仅是做OLL和PLL的区别时突然想到的,那就是:

基本上, 甚至如果一个公式集的设定就是 ''值得'', 那也不代表它每一次都值得.

有一些特定的情况在使用那些 ''不那么高级的'' 方式时其实也会产生一个更高的成绩.

比如说, 虽然我觉得背U ZBLL是100%值得的, 但是也有很少数量的情况确实比平均要难, 并且这个公式与其他情况的相比不是那么好.

拿一个能用R' U R U' R' U' R U2 R D R' U' R D' R2' U' R还原的ZBLL举例吧 (如果你出于某种原因仍然不能倒着看公式的话,那么就用公式翻译器吧).

另外, 如果你还在 ''COLL'' 等级操作, 在实际还原中, 因为你不积极地寻找ZBLL开始, 你会有很少的停顿, 并且会做R2' D' R U2 R' D R U2 R后做一个U或U'的调整后进入一个Uperm的情况.

因为这个围绕着UFL角的1x2x2块是完整的, 并且这也很容易预测出接下来是一个Uperm.

不过, 如果你仍然在 ''COLL'' 等级进行还原, 那么在识别情况和公式的质量方面, 对于那种情况, 你也可能会错过其它的11种比它好很多的ZBLL.

理论上, 你 *可以* 尝试并且将两种混合起来, 但是再练习中, 每个尝试这么做的人最终几乎都会错过简单ZBLL情况的机会, 并且保持偏向 ''COLL'' 而不是偏向ZBLL.

同样地, 如果你在 ''ZBLL'' 阶段操作, 甚至尽管公式并不是很好, 但它仍然比做COLL > EPLL要快, 尽管你将它视为一个公式来学.

你可以坚持使用更基本的解法来处理糟糕的U ZBLL从而免去''头疼''的烦恼, 但这并不会改变这样一个事实: 平均而言, U ZBLL要优于U OLL > PLL或U COLL > EPLL.

另外, 你可以再尝试并在某种程度上得到 ''两全其美'' ( "best of both worlds"), 但是除了最容易识别的ZBLL情况之外, 你将很难将其融为完美的组合, 并且随着时间的流逝, 你实际上不会使用除了最容易识别的情况之外任何更好的方法.

-

为什么会这样?

精打细算 (Mental priming) !

精打细算是我在Speedcubing Solution Pro中讲很深入的一个东西, 从某种意义上说, 它是高级解法的 ''基本原理'' 之一.

-

实际上, ''精打细算'' 的概念是这样的想法: 即如果你在魔方达到某个特定状态时一直在寻找 (在这里插入某些高级的东西), 那么你的决策过程就不会很复杂, 因为你已经 ''精打细算'' 了自己去做高级的事情.

不过, 如果, 你尝试挑选一个好的情况, 但却使用不好的情况中基础的方式 (在这里插入某些高级的东西), 那么你的决策过程就会突然变得比之前复杂很多.

我见过的唯一一个能够在任何等级都能成功把这个完成的人就是Feliks, 但是

  1. Feliks有时甚至都会错过一些他 ''知道'' 的情况, 因为他的手速太快了, 并且也没有对某种特定的情况或几种情况的混合进行 ''精打细算''.
  2. 虽然说 ''天赋'' 什么的就是一堆废话, 但是Feliks几乎肯定比你更有天赋, 并且他可以发挥出比一般魔友更有效的 ''选择'' 能力.

这就是我推荐你 ''仅学习较好的组合'' 而不是 ''仅学习较好的情况'' 的原因.

让我们用下面这个假想的例子 (虽然我其实并不同意它).

很多人看起来都觉得使用VLS还原点O很糟糕, 不值得 (造成这种情况的真正原因是因为, 其实很少人学了全部的VLS公式, 虽然它几乎肯定是比ZBLL更有用的高级技术).

无论如何, 学习除了点O以为所有的VLS是一个你可以 ''挑选'' 而又不破坏你在还原中进行 ''精打细算'' 的一种方法.

为什么?

因为如果你在还原末槽, 并且你知道它会组合起来, 并且也看到了没有一个顶层的棱块色向是正确的, 这样你会非常轻易地知道你不会遇到一个VLS的情况.

简单来说, 我相信很多人放弃高级技术的原因是他们发现有时候他们会遇到一个不好的情况, 并且我认为这来自于对高级公式集所提供的内容的不成熟的看法.

不过, 有一些高级公式集绝对是不好的, 比如反小鱼ZBLL.

我绝对不相信平均来说反小鱼ZBLL比反小鱼OLL > PLL要好, 而且我认为这丝毫不受欢迎.

但是如果当我遇到了一个惊人的公式集, 比如T ZBLL, 虽然它比T OLL > PLL或T COLL > EPLL要好, 但那并不意味着你不会遇到一些不好的情况.

每一个存在的公式集, 无论是现在存在的还是将来会存在的, 都会有一些不好的情况.

~

不要因小失大, 不好的公式集存在, 确实是这样的, 并且他们应该被避免.

~

它的意思就是, 不要仅因为它包含不好的情况就妄下断语说这整个公式集都不好.

如果你将这个逻辑应用到OLL和PLL上, 那么OLL和PLL也不会值得学习并且我们就应该换回初级方法了.

不过, 我们不能做, 因为初级方法也有不好的情况 (比如, 第二层的棱块在它自己的位置上但是方向反了)

所以, 如果你将那个逻辑推得再远一点, 我们所有人就应该都退魔了.

(或者, 如果你不喜欢那样的话, 想象一个更大的图景, 然后看一看实际上森林是什么样子, 而不是你想要的样子.)

~

魔方术语

OLL 顶层色向 (Orientation of Last Layer)

PLL 顶层位置 (Permutation of Last Layer)

ZBLL 在顶层有十字的情况下一步还原魔方 (Zborowski-Bruchem Last Layer)

COLL 一步还原顶层角块的色向和位置 (Corners Of Last Layer)

EPLL 还原棱块位置 (Edge Permutation of Last Layer)

VLS 完成最后一组且相连的F2L后强制跳OLL, 又称RLS / VRLS (Valk Last Slot)

* ZBLL / * COLL / * EPLL / * OLL / * PLL 某种ZBLL / COLL / EPLL / OLL / PLL情况 (*指某情况名称)

点O 没有正确色向的棱块的OLL情况

Uperm 一种PLL情况, 即三棱换. Perm即Permutation (U Permutation of Last Layer)

Why People ''Give Up'' on Advanced Alg Sets &

Advanced CFOP in General

Jayden McNeill – https://www.jaydenmcneillcubing.com/

Whether it's VLS, ZBLL, Pseudoslotting, or even F<RU>F' stuff, the vast majority of people who dip their feet into such solving styles eventually give up for a variety of reasons 

 

Sometimes the reasons are valid, for example if they know for a fact they could simply work on planning cross+1 or improve their fingertricks, which will have a much much better ROI compared to say, mastering Sune ZBLL

 

However, there's one key reason that kind of struck me all of a sudden while I was in the process of filming the first batch of "Less is more" episodes and thinking about the difference between doing ZBLL for 2GLL H VS just doing OLL & PLL

 

 

Basically, even if an alg set is "worth it", that doesn't mean it's ALWAYS worth it EVERY SINGLE TIME

 

 

There are going to be specific case scenarios where using the "less advanced" style would actually yield a better result 

 

For example, even though I think U ZBLL is 100% worth it, there are a small number of cases where the recognition is a little bit harder than average, and the algorithm isn't as great compared to other cases 

 

Take the ZBLL that gets solved with R' U R U' R' U' R U2 R D R' U' R D' R2' U' R for example (use algorithm translator if you still can't read algs backwards for some reason)

 

Anyways, if you were still operating at the "COLL" level, in a real solve you'd have much less of a pause since you're not actively looking for ZBLL, and you would do R2' D' R U2 R' D R U2 R into U or U' into Uperm

 

Because the 1x2x2 around the UFL corner gets preserved, it's also very easy to predict the Uperm

 

However, if you were still operating at the "COLL" level, you'd also miss out on being able to do the other 11 ZBLL cases for that set which are a LOT nicer, both in terms of recognition and algorithm quality 

 

In theory, you *could* try and incorporate a mix of both, but in practice, everyone who tries to do this eventually misses out on doing easy ZB cases and keep a "COLL" bias rather than a ZBLL one 

 

Also, if you're operating at the "ZBLL" level, even though the alg isn't amazing, it's still faster than doing COLL>EPLL even if you learn COLL>EPLL as an alg

 

You can save yourself the "heartache" of dealing with crappy U ZBLL's by sticking to a more basic solving style, but that doesn't change the fact that, on average, U ZBLL is better than U OLL>PLL or U COLL>EPLL

 

And again, you could try and get the "best of both worlds" to an extent, but beyond the easiest to recognize ZBLL cases, you're going to have a hard time incorporating the perfect combination and over time you won't really be using anything beyond the easiest to recognize cases 

 

~

 

 

Why is this?

 

Mental priming!

 

Mental priming is something I go very in depth into in Speedcubing Solution Pro, and in a sense, it's one of the "fundamentals" of advanced solving 

 

 

~

 

What "mental priming" really is as a concept is the idea that if you're always looking for (insert X advanced thing here) any time you reach a certain state on the cube, then your decision making process won't be complicated, as you've "mentally primed" yourself to do the advanced thing 

 

If, however, you try to pick and choose good cases, but use a basic style for bad cases within (insert X advanced thing here), then your decision making process is suddenly a LOT more complicated 

 

The only cuber I've seen pull this off with any level of success is Feliks, but

 

  1.  Even Feliks misses cases he "knows" because he's either going too fast, isn't mentally primed for that particular solve, or a mix of both
  2.  Even though natural talent is mostly a load of bullshit, Feliks is almost definitely more naturally talented than you are, and it likely plays into his ability to "pick and choose" more effectively than the average cuber

 

This is why instead of trying to "only learn the good cases", instead, I recommend you "only learn the good SETS"

 

Let's use the following hypothetical example (even though I don't actually agree with it)

 

With VLS, many people seem to think that the dot cases suck and aren't worth it (the real reason why this is, is because very few people have actually learned full VLS even though it's almost definitely a more useful advanced technique than ZBLL)

 

Regardless, learning full VLS minus the dot cases is one way you could "pick and choose" without wrecking your ability to be mentally primed in solves

 

Why?

 

Because if you're making your final pair, and know it's going to be a built pair, and also see that none of your LL edges are oriented, it's VERY EASY TO FIGURE OUT THAT YOU'RE NOT GOING TO GET A VLS CASE YOU KNOW

 

In short, I believe a lot of people give up on advanced techniques because they realize that sometimes they get a bad case, and I think this comes from an immature view of what advanced algorithm sets have to offer

 

Some advanced sets however are absolutely bad, like Antisune ZBLL for example

 

I don't believe for a second that Antisune ZBLL is better than Antisune OLL>PLL on average, and I don't think that's a hot take in the slightest 

 

Even when it comes to AMAZING sets like T ZBLL however, even though it's better than T OLL>PLL or T COLL>EPLL, that doesn't mean there are NO BAD CASES YOU CAN POSSIBLY GET 

 

EVERY ALGORITHM SET THAT HAS EXISTED, CURRENTLY EXISTS, OR WILL EXIST IN THE FUTURE, WILL HAVE SOME BAD CASES 

 

~

 

Don't miss the forrest for the trees. Bad algorithm sets exist, yes, and they should be avoided

 

~

 

That being said, don't assume that a whole set is bad just because it has bad cases 

 

If you applied that logic to OLL and PLL, then OLL and PLL wouldn't be worth it and we should all just switch back to the beginners method

 

Except we can't even do that, because the beginner method ALSO HAS BAD CASES (for example, the edge insert where the 2nd layer edge is flipped in place)

 

So, applying that logic further, we should all just quit cubing

 

(or if you don't like the sound of that, think more big picture, and actually see the forest for what it is instead of what you want it to be)

 

~
